name: ARIA attribute is defined in WAI-ARIA
description: This rule checks that each `aria-` attribute specified is defined in ARIA 1.2.
applicability_text: This rule applies to any attribute that starts with `aria-`.
expectations: Each target attribute is defined in [WAI-ARIA Specifications][].

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: This `article` element has an `aria-atomic` attribute which is defined in [WAI-ARIA Specifications][].
examples.code: <article aria-atomic="true">This is a description of something cool...</article>

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: This `div` element with a role of `dialog` has an `aria-modal` attribute which is defined in [WAI-ARIA Specifications][].
examples.code: Contains modal content...

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: This `div` element with a role of `textbox` has multiple `aria-` attributes which are defined in [WAI-ARIA Specifications][].

examples.name: Passed Example 4
examples.description: This `input` element with a type of `number` has multiple `aria-` attributes specified which are all defined in [WAI-ARIA Specifications][].
examples.code:
<label for="spinbutton">Enter a number between 0 and 100:</label>
<input id="spinbutton" aria-valuemax="100" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuenow="25" type="number" value="25" />

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: This `div` element with a role of `checkbox` has an `aria-not-checked` attribute which is not defined in [WAI-ARIA Specifications][].

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: This `div` element with a role of `searchbox` has an `aria-labelled` attribute (instead of `aria-labelledby`) which is not defined in [WAI-ARIA Specifications][].

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.description: This `canvas` element does not have an `aria-` attribute specified.

background: The presence of unknown ARIA attributes is often the result of a typo or other developer error. These attributes are ignored by browsers and other assistive technologies. This often means that a state or property which should exist is missing.
accessibility_support: There are no accessibility support issues known.
